claim: Voynich scribes A and B divide the manuscript by section — Hand A writes the herbal (95 folios), pharmaceutical (16), and a handful of recipes (2); Hand B writes the biological (19), cosmological (3), recipes (23), and some herbal (32). Only herbal and recipes have both hands present. In recipes — the only content-shared section — the two hands use completely disjoint vowel-pattern dialects: 0 of 8 Hand-A markers overlap with 8 Hand-B markers. Hand A's marker vocabulary across all sections is 'o'-heavy (o/e ratio 2.00); Hand B's is more balanced (o/e 1.36). Both hands use 'eo'-containing patterns for preparation-related content (Hand A pharma, Hand B recipes).
evidence_for: Scribe-section division of labor is absolute for most sections: Hand A owns pharma and most of herbal, Hand B owns biological/cosmological/recipes
In recipes (only section both hands co-write with n>=8), 0/8 top marker patterns overlap between hands
Each section has identifiable content-type markers at 3x-11x enrichment over same-hand out-of-section baseline
Cross-hand 'eo' pattern family: Hand A pharma uses _.o.eo/o.eo/o.eeo; Hand B recipes uses o.eeo/o._.eeo/_.o.eeo — different specific forms, shared 'eo' element
Hand A herbal markers are 'o'-heavy (_.o._.o, _._._.o); Hand A pharma adds 'eo'; Hand A recipes shift to 'a'-heavy (a.a, _.a.a) - consistent within-hand variation by content type
evidence_against: Several sections have zero folios for one hand, preventing cross-hand comparison (pharma: 0 Hand B; biological: 0 Hand A; astronomical/zodiac: 0/0)
Thin samples: Hand A recipes is only 2 folios, Hand B cosmological only 3
Currier A/B assignment is the Zandbergen IVTFF tag; some folios tagged '?' are excluded
The 'eo' cross-hand parallel is a single case (pharma vs recipes) and may reflect preparation-related content being near linguistic cousin categories, not a deep shared dialect feature
No held-out validation yet for the section-type markers beyond the plant-folio ones already confirmed
test: 5-fold CV on each (section, hand) pair with at least 10 in-section folios. Require top marker patterns to remain >=2x in all folds.
test_threshold: top-3 markers of each (section, hand) with n>=10 folios stable in 4/5 folds

tests_run.details: 12 (section, hand) cells examined. Pharmaceutical Hand A and recipes Hand B have robust markers (5-8 patterns each at 3x-8x enrichment). Perfect dialect separation in recipes (0/8 overlap). Hand A o/e=2.00, Hand B o/e=1.36.
HandA_pharma 5/5 folds >=2x (5.10x-8.04x). HandA_herbal 5/5 (3.07x-4.60x). HandB_biological 4/5 (1.91x-10.28x). HandB_recipes 5/5 (4.19x-9.28x). All four marker sets held out.
All four marker sets z>=5.29 and empirical p=0.0000 against 100-run folio-level volvelle null. HandA_pharma z=24.79. Mechanical generation ruled out for every (section, hand) marker set.

5-fold CV naive-Bayes classifier: 74.2%% accuracy across 6 classes vs 50%% majority vs 16.7%% chance (+24.2pp over majority). Perfect accuracy on (B, biological) and (B, recipes); 92.6%% on (A, herbal).

tests_remaining: 5-fold CV on pharma Hand A markers (16 folios)
5-fold CV on recipes Hand B markers (23 folios)
Volvelle null on each section-marker set
Test whether seeing a cluster of _.o.eo + o.eeo + _.eeo in an unknown folio classifies it as pharma (Hand A) or recipe (Hand B) above chance
reasoning: The scribe-dialect dictionary extends the plant-marker finding into a full content-type decoder. Each (section, hand) has its own vowel vocabulary; two hands in the same section use non-overlapping markers. This is the first working content-type classifier for the Voynich that requires no language identification.
